findings.description: Substring matching (pattern.lower() in text_lower) for short codes like 'CPI', 'PPI', 'GDP' (lines 26-27) could produce false positives in natural language text. Not a security issue — heuristic quality concern only. The first-match-wins approach means asset ordering in _ASSET_PATTERNS affects results.

_ASSET_PATTERNS and directional keyword lists are module-level constants. No injection risk — all patterns are hardcoded. Regex search targets are user/LLM text but patterns are fixed, preventing ReDoS.

estimate_risk_level() and estimate_time_window() use simple float comparisons and dict lookups — no injection surface. Input validation is implicit (type hints only, no runtime checks on confidence range 0-1).

notes: Pure heuristic module with no LLM calls, no I/O, no external data. Regex patterns are hardcoded (no user-controlled regex). Unicode handling is correct via Python's str.lower(). Substring matching false positives are a data quality concern, not a security concern.
